issues.detail: The skill requires Python 3.9+ with openpyxl and pandas, which is a minor platform assumption.

impact.impact_note: skill is general pandas/openpyxl patterns broadly known

scenarios.name: Deduplicate customers.xlsx and split into active/inactive sheets
scenarios.rationale: Treatment follows the documented 5-step flow (load + print shape, isnull/duplicated assessment, drop_duplicates(subset), and a single pd.ExcelWriter with engine='openpyxl' writing two named sheets); baseline does dedup and split correctly but skips the shape/quality preflight and writes two separate files instead of one workbook.

scenarios.name: Build a region-by-month revenue pivot from sales.csv
scenarios.rationale: Treatment uses pd.read_csv, pd.to_datetime(errors='coerce'), and df.pivot_table with values/index/columns/aggfunc='sum' and margins=True exactly as shown, then exports via ExcelWriter; baseline produces a pivot but omits margins=True totals and the explicit to_datetime guard against bad dates.
